destinations.evidence: The canonical shared payload validator enforces input-size, precompile-specific routing, URL/model/message checks, base executor fields, zero executor rejection, and TTL bounds.
The builder validates the detected async precompile payload with the shared validate_async_payload routine before decoding executor and TTL into a commitment.
RPC admission runs parent-state simulation, returns Ok for non-async transactions, then applies the shared static validator and stateful executor/wallet checks before pool insertion.

equivalence_argument: Both RPC admission and builder commitment construction call ritual_precompile_codecs::validate_async_payload, so oversize, executor, TTL, HTTP URL, and LLM model/message decisions share one validator before state-specific checks.
notes: The v2 port moved the shared validator to ritual-precompile-codecs rather than the old ritual-async crate.
rationale: The commit extracts core async payload checks into ritual_async::validation and changes RPC validate_async_payload to delegate to that module, matching the stated goal of RPC-builder parity. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async payload validation must reject inputs larger than MAX_ASYNC_INPUT_SIZE, zero executor addresses, ttl equal to zero, ttl greater than MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, HTTP requests with empty or non-http(s) URLs, and LLM requests with empty model or empty messages.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run the same corpus of HTTP, LLM, and base executor precompile inputs through ritual_async::validation::validate_async_payload and the RPC validator wrapper; assert both paths return equivalent success or mapped error for oversize inputs, executor zero, TTL bounds, HTTP URL validity, and LLM model/messages checks.
summary: Shared async payload validation gates RPC and builder.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run the same corpus of HTTP, LLM, and base executor precompile inputs through ritual_async::validation::validate_async_payload and the RPC validator wrapper; assert both paths return equivalent success or mapped error for oversize inputs, executor zero, TTL bounds, HTTP URL validity, and LLM model/messages checks.
title: Async payload validation is shared across RPC and builder
